untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of 'price reaching new highs' and 'volume surges without corresponding upside progress' — these require multi-bar lookback logic and comparative analysis (new highs vs. prior period) that cannot be expressed as single 3-field conditions with allowed indicators. Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) could approximate new highs but do not capture the volume-price relationship strength required. The price-to-volume ratio threshold (0.3% per 1M shares) requires custom derived metrics not in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation using vol_ratio_20 as a proxy for volume percentile comparisons and returns for price progress vs. volume. However, the core logic of 'new highs on low volume' (weak demand signal) and 'volume surge without price progress' requires pattern recognition and comparative metrics beyond the scope of simple indicator thresholds. A full implementation would need: (1) new high detection logic, (2) volume surge detection, and (3) price-to-volume efficiency calculation as custom indicators.
